has experienced a recent major branch failure involving a lead approximately 12 inches in diameter. The canopy is heavily impacted by wisteria, which covers an estimated 90% of the crown and contributes additional weight and stress to the tree. The tree also contains a large lead extending over the residence that exhibits natural lion-tailing, increasing the risk of future limb failure. Due to the recent failure history, structural concerns, and proximity to the home, removal is recommended.Reason for Removal:Dying tree
